metadata.title: Guideline on the clinical evaluation of antifungal agents for the treatment and prophylaxis of invasive fungal disease

rules.rule_text: Before and during the clinical development programme for an antifungal agent, the following must be investigated: spectrum of in-vitro antifungal activity, mode of action, mechanism(s) of resistance, cross-resistance within and between drug classes, synergy/antagonism with agents of different classes, efficacy in animal models, and PK/PD relationship.
rules.verbatim_quote: Before and during the clinical development programme for an antifungal agent it is expected that efforts will be made to investigate the following: Spectrum of in-vitro antifungal activity. Mode of action. Mechanism(s) of resistance. Cross-resistance within and between anti-fungal drug classes. Synergy or antagonism with antifungal agents of different classes. Efficacy in animal models. Pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic (PK/PD) relationship.

rules.rule_text: All fungi isolated and considered causative of IFD must be forwarded to one or more designated reference laboratories for confirmation of identity (including sequence-based methods) and susceptibility testing.
rules.verbatim_quote: All fungi that are isolated and considered to be causative of IFD should be forwarded to one or more designated reference laboratories for confirmation of identity (including sequence-based methods) and susceptibility testing.

rules.rule_text: At least some of the in-vitro data should be generated using EUCAST susceptibility testing methodologies. Any available EUCAST-recommended breakpoints for common Candida species should be included in the SmPC.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that at least some of the in-vitro data should be generated using susceptibility testing methodologies published by EUCAST since this will facilitate the setting of EUCAST-recommended breakpoints. Any available EUCAST-recommended susceptibility testing breakpoints for common Candida species should be included in the SmPC.

rules.rule_text: Patients in IFD studies should be categorised according to EORTC/MSG definitions for proven, probable, and possible IFD. The most recent genus/species-specific definitions must be applied.
rules.source_section: 4.2.2 Categorisation of patients according to laboratory confirmation of the diagnosis
rules.verbatim_quote: In the analyses of outcomes, patients should be categorised according to the EORTC/MSG definitions for proven, probable and possible IFD that have been derived for the purposes of clinical and epidemiological research. The most recent recommendations should be followed, applying genus/species-specific definitions as appropriate.

rules.rule_text: An independent expert panel (preferably independent of study personnel) unaware of treatment assignments should assess certainty of IFD diagnosis in individual patients. The panel's decisions should be used to derive the population for the primary analysis of outcomes.
rules.source_section: 4.2.2 Categorisation of patients according to laboratory confirmation of the diagnosis
rules.verbatim_quote: In all clinical efficacy studies of antifungal agents for IFD it is recommended that an expert panel (preferably independent of study personnel) that is unaware of treatment assignments should assess the certainty of diagnosis in individual patients and that the decisions of the panel should be used to derive the population included in the primary analysis of outcomes.

rules.rule_text: The active comparative regimen should be restricted to a single regimen whenever possible. The chosen regimen should be one of the optimal available therapies for the type of IFD under study.
rules.verbatim_quote: Whenever possible the active comparative therapy should be restricted to a single regimen. This may be a single agent throughout or a single initial parenteral agent followed by a single oral agent. The chosen regimen should be one of the optimal available therapies for the type of IFD to be treated in an individual study.
rules.exceptions: Allowing investigators limited choice regarding comparative regimen (e.g. choice of amphotericin B preparation) may be unavoidable.

rules.rule_text: The protocol must pre-define a minimum duration of therapy for patient evaluability and a maximum duration beyond which patients who have not met response criteria should be considered to have failed therapy.
rules.verbatim_quote: The protocol should pre-define a minimum duration of therapy for patient evaluability and a maximum duration beyond which patients who have not met the response criteria should be considered to have failed therapy.

rules.rule_text: For combination antifungal therapy, the selection of combination regimens should be supported by demonstration of benefit for co-administration over each agent given alone in an animal model, if possible.
rules.verbatim_quote: if possible, the selection of combination regimens to treat specific types of IFD should also be supported by a demonstration of benefit for co-administration over each agent given alone in an animal model.

rules.rule_text: Data from at least one randomised, double-blind study comparing test and reference antifungal regimens is normally necessary to demonstrate a satisfactory risk-benefit relationship for use in a specific type of IFD.
rules.source_section: 4.2.4 Issues for study design — Randomised controlled studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Data from at least one randomised and double blind study that compares test and reference antifungal regimens would normally be considered necessary to demonstrate a satisfactory risk-benefit relationship for use of an agent in a specific type of IFD.
rules.exceptions: If no comparative agent is approved or widely efficacious, a superiority design against 'best available' may be appropriate.

rules.rule_text: Studies should be adequately powered to demonstrate at least non-inferiority for the test versus reference regimen. If no approved comparator exists for a specific IFD type, a superiority design against 'best available' reference may be appropriate.
rules.source_section: 4.2.4 Issues for study design — Randomised controlled studies
rules.verbatim_quote: These studies should be of adequate power to demonstrate at least non-inferiority for the test versus reference regimen using an appropriate value of delta... If there is no comparative agent approved for treating a specific type of IFD or there is no comparative regimen widely held to be adequately efficacious... then it may be appropriate to seek to demonstrate superiority of the test regimen versus the 'best available' reference regimen.

rules.rule_text: Alternative non-randomised study designs may be used only in exceptional circumstances. If used, the design should still include a randomisation step since an internal control group makes interpretation considerably more reliable.
rules.source_section: 4.2.4 Issues for study design — Other study designs
rules.verbatim_quote: Alternative study designs may be used only in exceptional circumstances and must be very carefully justified... It is strongly recommended that any alternative study design should still include a randomisation step because the availability of an internal control group makes the interpretation of the outcomes considerably more reliable
rules.exceptions: Prospective non-comparative studies with external/historical controls may support conditional approval in exceptional cases.

rules.rule_text: The timing of the TOC visit should reflect the type of IFD under study, taking into account EORTC/MSG recommendations, terminal elimination half-lives, expected recovery from neutropenia, and other relevant factors. Patients with deep-seated Candida or Aspergillus infections should usually be followed for 2-3 months post-therapy.
rules.verbatim_quote: The timing of the test of cure (TOC) assessments on which the primary analysis of outcomes will be based should reflect the type of IFD under study... Patients with deep-seated infections with Candida or Aspergillus should usually be followed up for two or three months post-therapy.
rules.exceptions: Shorter follow-up may be acceptable in specific types (e.g. when acute IFD is followed by long-term prophylaxis).

rules.rule_text: Outcomes must be assessed using EORTC/MSG response criteria or adequately justified alternative criteria. Clinical and mycological components of global outcomes must be presented separately, and discrepancies noted and discussed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Outcomes should be assessed in all randomised and treated patients using the response criteria recommended by the EORTC/MSG or, if adequately justified, using alternative criteria. The clinical and mycological components of global outcomes should also be presented separately and any discrepancies should be noted and discussed.

rules.rule_text: All studies (including double-blind) should include assessment of clinical, mycological, and global outcomes by a panel of experts independent of the study and unaware of treatment assignments. The primary analysis should preferably be based on this independent panel's outcomes.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that all studies (i.e. even if double blind) should include an assessment of clinical, mycological and global outcomes by a panel of experts that is independent of the study and unaware of treatment assignments. It is preferable that the primary analysis should be based on outcomes determined by such an independent panel

rules.rule_text: Clinical development programmes must include assessment of population pharmacokinetics in patients. The data should assess any relationship between plasma levels and outcomes, and pharmacogenomic polymorphisms affecting PK should be taken into account.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that clinical development programmes will include an assessment of population pharmacokinetics in patients. These data should be used to assess whether any relationship is demonstrable between plasma levels and outcomes. Advances in pharmacogenomics should also be taken into account

rules.rule_text: Salvage therapy studies in refractory IFD should be conducted only after satisfactory efficacy has been shown in one or more specific IFD types. Patients must have proven IFD (proven and probable for aspergillosis) that has persisted or progressed despite previous antifungal therapy.
rules.verbatim_quote: As a general rule studies of clinical efficacy in refractory IFD should be conducted only after satisfactory efficacy has been shown for an antifungal agent in one or more specific types of IFD... Studies of clinical efficacy in refractory IFD should enrol patients with proven IFD (except that proven and probable cases of invasive aspergillosis might be acceptable) that has persisted or progressed despite previous antifungal therapy.

rules.rule_text: An indication for empiric therapy in neutropenic patients with fever is not tenable since it would wrongly imply efficacy against any type of IFD that may be present. Such composite endpoint studies cannot assess treatment efficacy of an antifungal agent.
rules.source_section: 4.4 Studies in patients with febrile neutropenia
rules.verbatim_quote: an indication for empiric therapy is not tenable since it would wrongly imply that the antifungal agent has been shown to be (or could be expected to be) effective in the treatment of any type of IFD that may be present in febrile neutropenic patients.

rules.rule_text: Prophylaxis studies for IFD should only be conducted after an agent has demonstrated satisfactory clinical efficacy in treatment of several types of IFD. At least one randomised, comparative study with sufficient statistical power is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that studies that assess the use of an antifungal agent for prophylaxis of IFD would be conducted only after an agent has demonstrated satisfactory clinical efficacy in the treatment of several types of IFD... At least one randomised, comparative study with sufficient statistical power to demonstrate superiority or exclude inferiority of the investigational regimen versus an appropriate active comparative regimen
rules.exceptions: In certain patient populations, demonstrating superiority against placebo may be necessary.

rules.rule_text: Adequate steps must be taken to exclude patients who already have IFD before enrolment in prophylaxis studies. The criteria for defining patients at risk of IFD must be specified in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Adequate steps should be taken to exclude patients who may already have an IFD before enrolment. It is essential that the criteria by which patients are defined as being at risk of IFD should be specified in the protocol and documented in the study report.

rules.rule_text: The primary efficacy analysis in prophylaxis studies should compare incidences of proven or probable IFD (EORTC/MSG definitions) during the treatment period and for a defined period after cessation of prophylaxis.
rules.verbatim_quote: The primary efficacy analysis should compare the incidences of any proven or probable IFD (using the EORTC/MSG definitions as in treatment studies) between treatments. Incidences of IFD should be compared during the treatment period and for a defined period after cessation of prophylaxis using data from all treated patients.

rules.rule_text: Efficacy demonstrated in adults in specific clinical circumstances may be extrapolated for use in children. Paediatric studies should evaluate PK and safety of the novel agent in children with IFD, with clinical and mycological outcomes collected for exploratory analyses.
rules.verbatim_quote: a demonstration of efficacy in adults in specific circumstances may be extrapolated to use in the same circumstances in children... studies should aim to evaluate the pharmacokinetics and safety of the novel agent in children with IFD. However, information on clinical and mycological outcomes should be collected as in studies in adults and should be subjected to exploratory analyses.

rules.rule_text: Safety data derived from comparative studies in which at least one treatment group receives the investigational agent with no other antifungal agent is essential. Post-marketing safety update reports with specific studies to evaluate particular issues raised by pre-authorisation data may be required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety data derived from comparative studies in which at least one treatment group receives the investigational antifungal agent with no other antifungal agent can help identify adverse reactions and it is essential that such data are generated during the clinical development programme.

rules.rule_text: It is not appropriate to grant an indication for fungaemia (e.g. Candidaemia) or catheter-related fungaemia alone. Fungaemia including catheter-associated cases should be included within indications for Invasive aspergillosis or Invasive candidiasis.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is not considered appropriate to grant an indication for fungaemia (e.g. Candidaemia) or for catheter-related fungaemia... use in fungaemia, including fungaemia associated with catheters, is considered to be included in terms such as Invasive aspergillosis or Invasive candidiasis.

rules.rule_text: Indications for prophylaxis should be separated from indications for treatment. Prophylaxis indications may be qualified by specific patient populations and specific fungi.
rules.verbatim_quote: Indications for prophylaxis should be separated from indications for treatment. If appropriate, indications for prophylaxis may be qualified by specific patient populations and specific fungi.

rules.rule_text: Indications for salvage therapy must strictly reflect the range of antifungal agents to which patients enrolled with refractory IFD had previously been exposed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Indications for use in salvage therapy should strictly reflect the range of antifungal agents to which patients enrolled with refractory IFD had previously been exposed.
